plan.original_text: JPM's EPS will decline by more than 5% YoY in Q3 2024 compared to Q3 2023 if credit quality metrics worsen significantly.
plan.thesis: JPMorgan should outperform over the next 4–6 quarters if net interest income remains resilient as the rate cycle peaks and credit quality holds up better than market fears. The bank's scale, diversified revenue streams (investment banking, markets, wealth management), and best-in-class efficiency provide multiple levers for earnings stability. The thesis fails if a hard landing accelerates loan loss provisions beyond consensus expectations or if investment banking recovery disappoints.

steps.description: JPM quarterly net income history — fundamental.history.net_income
JPM net income year-over-year growth rate
JPM quarterly earnings and EPS history — fundamental.earnings.history
Delinquency Rate on Credit Card Loans, All Commercial Banks — FRED:DRCCLACBS
Trend of credit card delinquency rate
JPM news articles regarding credit quality, loan loss provisions, and EPS impact — sentiment.news.articles
Assess whether JPM's EPS will decline by more than 5% YoY in Q3 2024 if credit quality worsens significantly

ambiguity_notes.EPS: Used net income history as a proxy for EPS timeseries to compute growth, and fetched earnings history text to get exact EPS numbers.
ambiguity_notes.credit quality metrics: Used FRED:DRCCLACBS (Delinquency Rate on Credit Card Loans) as a macro proxy for credit quality worsening, and fetched news articles for JPM-specific loan loss provision context.

plan.reasoning: To evaluate the conditional statement about JPM's EPS declining >5% YoY due to worsening credit quality, we fetch JPM's net income history to compute YoY growth and its earnings history text for exact EPS figures. We also fetch the macro credit card delinquency rate to compute its trend as a proxy for credit quality worsening, and gather recent news on JPM's loan loss provisions to assess the overall impact on earnings.
